desc: Tallinn mixes a storybook medieval center with Nordic design energy and seaside districts, where long summer evenings and very short winter days define the pace.
hls: Compact city ideal for 2–4 day trips
Strong digital infrastructure and remote-work ease
Big seasonal daylight contrast on the Gulf of Finland

todo.name: Old Town upper/lower loop
todo.desc: Walk Toompea viewpoints, city walls, and lower-town squares for Tallinn's signature medieval skyline.

todo.name: Telliskivi creative quarter
todo.desc: Browse design shops, food halls, and street art in repurposed industrial spaces.

todo.name: Seaplane Harbour museum
todo.desc: Use this interactive museum to connect Tallinn's naval and Baltic-sea story.

todo.name: Kalamaja + Noblessner walk
todo.desc: Combine wooden-house streets with modern waterfront redevelopment and cafés.

todo.name: Kadriorg & KUMU
todo.desc: Pair imperial-era park grounds with Estonia's leading art museum.

todo.name: Seasonal planning block
todo.desc: In Nov–Jan, front-load outdoor sights in midday light and reserve evenings for saunas and dining.

wifi.note: Excellent citywide connectivity; Estonia is one of Europe's easiest bases for digital work.

prac.fltNote: Tallinn Airport is close to center with efficient tram access and frequent links to major European hubs.
prac.visa: Estonia is in Schengen; standard Schengen entry rules apply.
alerts: November–January daylight is very limited; some outdoor itineraries feel rushed without planning.

sweet: June to August offers long daylight and the most comfortable old-town sightseeing conditions.
